Title: Flavonoid-mediated bacterial spermidine biosynthesis enhances vitamin accumulation in tomato fruits

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Microbial metabolite that promotes vitamin C and vitamin B6 accumulation in tomato fruits; associated with plant growth, development, and stress resistance
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Bacterial culture of L. soli P18; rhizosphere-associated bacterial metabolite acting on tomato fruits
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; identified as key spermidine biosynthetic enzyme gene upregulated by flavonoids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes bacterial spermidine biosynthesis, which in turn enhances vitamin C and vitamin B6 accumulation in tomato fruits
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Metagenomic sequencing; prokaryotic transcriptomics; bacterial metabolite profiling; spermidine quantification
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated in flavonoid-treated L. soli P18 and in flavonoid-treated rhizosphere metagenomes
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SpeD abundance/expression increased in flavonoid-treated rhizosphere samples and in L. soli P18. 2. Upregulation was associated with increased spermidine production. 3. Increased bacterial spermidine was linked to higher vitamin C and vitamin B6 in tomato fruits.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential microbial engineering target to boost spermidine production for tomato fruit biofortification
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Metagenomics: PRJNA1182265; Bacterial transcriptomics: PRJNA1182113; Widely targeted metabolomics: MTBLS13506
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Function inferred from canonical spermidine pathway and supported by transcript/metabolite association rather than direct enzyme knockout of speD

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Microbial metabolite that promotes vitamin C and vitamin B6 accumulation in tomato fruits; associated with plant growth, development, and stress resistance
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Bacterial culture of L. soli P18; rhizosphere-associated bacterial metabolite acting on tomato fruits
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; identified as key spermidine biosynthetic enzyme gene upregulated by flavonoids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes bacterial spermidine production, leading to increased vitamin C and vitamin B6 accumulation in tomato fruits
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Metagenomic sequencing; prokaryotic transcriptomics; bacterial metabolite profiling; spermidine quantification
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated in flavonoid-treated L. soli P18 and in flavonoid-treated rhizosphere metagenomes
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SpeE abundance/expression increased after flavonoid treatment. 2. SpeE upregulation coincided with elevated spermidine accumulation in L. soli P18. 3. Bacterially produced spermidine enhanced tomato fruit vitamin C and B6 content.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful microbial engineering target to enhance spermidine-mediated fruit nutritional quality
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Metagenomics: PRJNA1182265; Bacterial transcriptomics: PRJNA1182113; Widely targeted metabolomics: MTBLS13506
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Function inferred from canonical spermidine pathway and supported by transcript/metabolite association rather than direct enzyme knockout of speE

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant and nutritional vitamins beneficial to human health
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Bacterial twitching motility, rhizosphere colonization, and spermidine biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Twitching motility-related genes including pilus assembly/biogenesis and chemosensory pili genes; SpeD; SpeE
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates or positively regulates expression of twitching motility-related genes and spermidine biosynthesis-related genes under flavonoid treatment
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Root-derived flavonoids, especially combined rutin+naringenin chalcone+kaempferol-3-O-rutinoside
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Dual regulator linking flavonoid perception to Lysobacter colonization and spermidine production, thereby decisively enabling vitamin accumulation in tomato fruits
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes tomato fruit vitamin C and vitamin B6 accumulation indirectly by enhancing L. soli P18 colonization and spermidine production
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene knockout (ΔRpoN); complementation (ΔRpoN-C); motility assays; root colonization assay; bacterial spermidine quantification; plant fruit vitamin profiling; co-expression network
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Hub gene positively associated with twitching motility genes and speD/speE in flavonoid-treated L. soli P18
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Flavonoid treatment in natural or sterilized soil and bacterial culture
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RpoN was identified as a flavonoid-responsive hub regulator in L. soli P18. 2. RpoN knockout reduced twitching motility and rhizosphere colonization under flavonoids, restored by complementation. 3. RpoN knockout also reduced spermidine production and abolished the bacterium's ability to enhance tomato fruit vitamin C and B6 accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Microbial engineering target for rhizosphere biostimulants to improve fruit nutritional quality
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Metagenomics: PRJNA1182265; Bacterial transcriptomics: PRJNA1182113; Fruit transcriptomics: PRJNA1182117; Widely targeted metabolomics: MTBLS13506; Flavonoid-targeted metabolomics: MTBLS13507
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts in bacterium, not tomato; metabolite effect measured in tomato fruit. Identified as dual regulator in study.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant and nutritional vitamins beneficial to human health
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Flavonoid biosynthesis in roots/root exudation, indirectly rhizosphere microbiome restructuring and fruit vitamin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Establishes elevated root flavonoid state that enables microbiome-dependent vitamin accumulation in fruits
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increases root/rhizosphere flavonoids and promotes vitamin C and vitamin B6 accumulation in fruits in natural soil but not sterilized soil
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression; root/rhizosphere flavonoid metabolomics; fruit vitamin profiling; soil sterilization comparison
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Overexpressed transgenic line with up-regulated flavonoid biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlMYB12 overexpression increased flavonoid accumulation in roots/rhizosphere. 2. Fruits of SlMYB12-overexpressing plants accumulated more vitamin C and vitamin B6 in natural soil. 3. This advantage disappeared in sterilized soil, indicating microbiome dependence.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be used to enhance beneficial root exudate flavonoids and recruit favorable microbiomes for fruit quality improvement
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Flavonoid-targeted metabolomics: MTBLS13507; Fruit widely targeted metabolomics: MTBLS13506; 16S amplicon sequencing: PRJNA1303084
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Primary role in this paper is host-side driver of flavonoid-mediated microbiome restructuring rather than direct regulation of vitamin biosynthetic genes in fruit
